key_patterns: PAT-054 — Exodus 28:15-21 (Urim and Thummim Step-Gate Pattern, Level 3, score 9.1): Twelve named stones = named step decision gates. FaultLocator = the oracle. @probe = the stone.
PAT-055 — Ezekiel 33:1-9 (Watchman Step-Sentinel Pattern, Level 3, score 8.9): Watchman at post = probe at step. ProbeAlert = trumpet alarm. Dark zone = post without watchman.
PAT-056 — 1 Kings 18:30-39 (Elijah Staged Evidence Pattern, Level 3, score 9.0): Three water pours = three StepReplay runs with deliberate parameter variation. Staged evidence accumulation.
PAT-057 — Nehemiah 3:1-5, 28-32 (Section-by-Section Wall Pattern, Level 2, score 8.4): Named section roster = ProbeMap coverage visualization.
PAT-058 — Numbers 9:15-23 (Cloud Step-Token Pattern, Level 2, score 8.2): Cloud step signal = halt_on_fault mechanism.
pain_point_solved: Multi-step LLM pipeline engineers cannot determine which step in a chain caused a production failure. chain-probe is the first framework-agnostic pip-installable library for step-level semantic fault isolation. It names the broken step, distinguishes origin faults from cascade failures, and enables frozen-input step replay for debugging.
competitive_moat: GREEN — no framework-agnostic pip-installable step-level fault isolation library found in 9 web searches. LangGraph time travel is LangGraph-locked. 'Deterministic replay for AI is a missing primitive' confirmed (sakurasky.com).

chain_probe_summary.core_api: @probe decorator + ProbeSession + FaultLocator + StepReplay + ProbeMap + CLI
chain_probe_summary.key_innovation: CASCADE fault analysis — distinguishes the step that CAUSED the failure from downstream steps that inherited bad input
chain_probe_summary.implementation_status: core_algorithm.py 550+ lines, README.md, architecture.md, api_spec.md, examples.md

next_cycle_directions: DIRECTION 1 — BEAT model-parity (8.90 ALL-TIME RECORD): Identify a pain point where the gap is as acute as 'you cannot migrate LLMs safely' or 'you cannot find which step broke.' Candidate: LLM runtime output schema enforcement with auto-correction (not just validation — enforcement). Research: does any pip library do schema-level enforcement with LLM output auto-retry? DeepEval validates output structure but does not auto-correct. Pydantic validates but requires structured output mode (not all LLMs support it). The gap: enforce + auto-correct non-conforming output at runtime without requiring structured output mode. Score this seriously.

DIRECTION 2 — SCRIPTURE: Mine 1 Kings 19 (the still small voice — signal in noise, relevance filtering), Ezekiel 1:4-28 (fiery chariot — multi-dimensional state representation, embedding spaces), Proverbs 25:2 ('It is the glory of God to conceal a matter; to search out a matter is the glory of kings' — the systematic investigation mandate maps perfectly to retrieval augmentation and knowledge discovery). These are HIGH POTENTIAL passages not yet touched.

DIRECTION 3 — IMPLEMENTATION SPRINT: chain-probe v0.1 needs to ship to PyPI (3-4 weeks). Resolve KU-037 (fault_score threshold calibration) as the blocking known unknown. Prototype the EmbeddingJudge with all-MiniLM-L6-v2 and validate fault detection against 3 known-bad RAG pipeline scenarios. Also: context-lens v0.1 and drift-guard v0.1 are both prototype-complete — ship them to PyPI. The pipeline has 10 tools. Time to start shipping.

notes: chain-probe addresses the most painful daily debugging experience for multi-step LLM pipeline engineers: you cannot tell which step broke. The Urim and Thummim gave Israel named, position-specific oracle answers. chain-probe gives engineers the same: named step checkpoints, step-level sentinels, and staged evidence replay. Elijah poured water three times — each pour a deliberate condition change. That is exactly what StepReplay does. The pattern is not metaphor. It is structure. And structure is buildable.
